items.title: Show HN: DejaView – Terminal dashboard for all your Claude Code sessions
items.why_it_matters: An open-source terminal dashboard called DejaView was released to let you easily manage and resume your Claude Code sessions. It groups your past transcripts by project and lets you jump back into them instantly, solving the annoyance of having to remember exactly which directory a session was started in. Run `uvx dejaview` in your terminal today to instantly see and resume your active AI-ingest coding sessions.

items.title: Show HN: Isitsecure – 1-command SAST and DAST and LLM security scanner for web apps
items.why_it_matters: A developer launched Isitsecure, a 1-command security scanner built specifically to find vulnerabilities in vibe-coded web applications where the AI might have introduced security flaws. It combines static and dynamic analysis to test your routes and database access points. Run this security scanner against your local Next.js and Supabase project today to catch and fix any vulnerabilities before you deploy.

items.title: Show HN: Kote – Capture and reuse engineering context from AI chats and Git
items.why_it_matters: A new open-source tool called Kote automatically captures and organizes engineering context from your AI chats (including Claude Code and Codex) and Git pushes, making it searchable via a web app or VS Code. This helps you keep track of architectural decisions and debugging steps without needing to manually document them. Install Kote to automatically log your next Claude Code session and Git commit for AI-ingest so you don't lose track of your progress.

items.title: Claude Code sends 33k tokens before reading the prompt; OpenCode sends 7k
items.why_it_matters: A comparative study revealed that Claude Code has significant token overhead, sometimes sending up to 33k tokens before reading a prompt due to inefficient caching and harness strategies, compared to only 7k tokens from OpenCode. This directly impacts your API costs and rate limits when vibe coding. Monitor your Claude Code token usage on your Anthropic console today, or try OpenCode for your next feature to see if it lowers your development costs.

items.title: Claude Code May–July 2026 weekly limits promotion
items.why_it_matters: Anthropic is running a weekly limits promotion for Claude Code from May through July 2026, giving developers higher rate limits to build and test projects. This gives you more runway to run intensive coding sessions without hitting daily or weekly usage walls. Check your Anthropic console's limits page today to see if the promotion is active for your account and use the extra headroom to run a long coding session.

items.title: Show HN: Let your coding agent iterate by seeing the browser
items.why_it_matters: A new tool called peek-cli allows your terminal-based coding agents to actually see the browser, enabling them to visually inspect, iterate, and debug frontend layouts. This bridges the gap for CLI agents that struggle to write accurate CSS and UI components without visual feedback. Install peek-cli and run it alongside Claude Code today to let the agent visually inspect and debug your Next.js frontend layout.

items.title: Show HN: Capn-hook for coding agents – don't grep the same mystery twice
items.why_it_matters: An open-source utility called capn-hook prevents coding agents from repeatedly running the same search and grep commands across your codebase, saving you time and API tokens. It intercepts repetitive agent behaviors and forces them to use cached or smarter search strategies. Add capn-hook to your AI-ingest repository this week to stop Claude Code from wasting your token budget on repetitive directory searches.

items.title: Show HN: Adaptive Recall, persistent memory for AI assistants over MCP
items.why_it_matters: Adaptive Recall is a new tool that provides persistent memory for AI assistants over the Model Context Protocol (MCP), allowing Claude to remember context, preferences, and project details across entirely different chat sessions. Connect Adaptive Recall to your local Claude setup this week to stop repeating your project's tech stack instructions to the AI.
